title: Mid session 2901 T225 A
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS

Term 2, 2025

MATH2901 Higher Theory of Statistics Mid-session test A

(1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

(2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

(3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

(4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

(5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

(6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided. You may use your own calculators. You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask if you need more paper.

All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.  Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 2

1. [10 marks] Let (X1 , . . . , Xn ) be mutually independent random variables with Xi ∼ Bin(ni , π), for some ni ∈ N (i = 1, . . . , n) and π ∈ (0, 1).

a) [4 marks] By using the properties of the moment generating function, determine the exact distribution of the sample total n X Sn = Xi . i=1

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 3

b) [4 marks] Provide an approximation of the distribution of Sn based on the Central Limit Theorem and valid when n is ‘large’. Justify carefully.

c) [2 marks] Would the previous approximation be valid if n is ‘small’ but the corresponding ni ’s are ‘large’ ? Explain.

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 4

2. [10 marks] Let (X1 , X2 ) be a bivariate discrete vector, with SX1 = {0, 1, 2}, SX2 = {0, 1, 2} and pX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = c(x1 + x2 + 1) for (x1 , x2 ) ∈ SX1 × SX2 and a constant c > 0.

a) [3 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.

b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 5

c) [2 marks] Find E(X1 ).

d) [3 marks] Find Cov(X1 , X2 ).

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 6

3. [10 marks] Suppose that the distribution of the diameter (in cm) of apples from orchard A is N (7.2, 0.52 ) and the distribution of the diameter (in cm) of apples from orchard B is N (6.8, 0.42 ). An apple is randomly selected from each orchard.

a) [3 marks] Find the probability that the apple from orchard A has a diameter greater than 8 cm.

b) [7 marks] Find the probability that the apple from orchard A has a diameter that exceeds the apple from orchard B by more than 1 cm.

(Hint: Φ(0.41) = 0.6591, Φ(0.6) = 0.7257, Φ(0.937) = 0.8256, Φ(1.463) = 0.9283, Φ(1.6) = 0.9452.)
